text: Migratory movement of human capital and socio-economic sustainability This publication explores the links between migration of human capital and the sustainability of socio-economic systems. Migrations – evolution and ambivalence proved the theory that, on the one hand, the influx of labor has always entailed pressure on national social welfare systems, whereas, on the other hand, the receptor countries (those with the main migrant inflows) are often characterized by quite high levels of socio-economic sustainability, with these countries actively competing for rare and highly qualified human capital. The publication contains the findings of an analysis of quantitative and qualitative aspects of the migration of human capital: the main factors, quantitative and qualitative characteristics, directions, trends and structure of migration and emigration in EU countries and Ukraine are introduced in the publication; the socio-demographic characteristics of migrants from EU countries and Ukraine before and after the outbreak of full-scale war in Ukraine are introduced in the publication. In the publication, the strengths and weaknesses, threats and opportunities for the migration of human capital and sustainable development are identified and compared in the receptor countries, while the implications of migrations for the implementation of the Sustainable development goals in receptor countries and in the countries making donations (donor countries) are identified.
